evals.prompt: Convert this raw Kibana release notes output into Elastic Observability 9.3.2 Stack release notes. File is at ./raw-rn-9.3.2.md. Use gh to fetch PR context and docs-content release-notes/elastic-observability for style.
evals.expected_output: A single fenced Markdown code block with ## 9.3.2 [elastic-observability-9.3.2-release-notes], two ### sections (Features and enhancements, Fixes), verb-first bullets with {{kib-pull}} PR links, plus a Notes (Confidence + Assumptions) section.
expectations: Output contains exactly one ## header and two ### headers in the required order
Bullets start with active verbs (Adds, Fixes, Improves, etc.) in present tense
Notes section explicitly calls out low-confidence bullets or assumptions
PR links use [#123456]({{kib-pull}}123456) format when PR numbers exist
No component sub-headings from the tool (e.g. Discover, Management) appear in the output

evals.prompt: I have raw Security release notes in the clipboard for 9.4.0. Turn them into Stack format for elastic-security. Some items have no PR number.
evals.expected_output: Elastic Security 9.4.0 release notes in the required structure, with items lacking PR numbers left without links and flagged in Notes.
expectations: Bullets without PR numbers do not include a [#]({{kib-pull}}...) link
Notes section mentions missing PR numbers or reduced confidence where applicable
UI labels are bolded; feature names capitalized (not bolded)

evals.prompt: Check the links in our Observability 9.2.1 release notes page and suggest fixes.
evals.expected_output: Skill does NOT generate full Stack release notes from scratch; it is for converting raw Kibana RN tool output. User asked for link checking, not RN conversion.
expectations: Does NOT output a full ## 9.2.1 [elastic-observability-9.2.1-release-notes] block with Features and Fixes
Either declines or suggests using crosslink-validator / the correct task; does not treat this as a Kibana RN conversion request

evals.prompt: The raw tool output has a 'Breaking changes' section and a 'Deprecations' section. Convert it to Stack Observability 9.5.0 format.
evals.expected_output: Release notes with only Features and enhancements and Fixes sections; breaking changes and deprecations mapped into those two sections, with mapping decisions explained in Notes.
expectations: Output has only two ### sections: Features and enhancements, then Fixes
No ### Breaking changes or ### Deprecations headings appear
Notes section records how breaking changes and deprecations were mapped
